mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the equity discount rate, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher when FEDFUNDS stays below 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate falling below 6 percent signals labor market tightening that accelerates consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative or flat when UNRATE crosses below 6.0 in more than 50 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and stable industrial production confirms robust consumer demand without supply constraints, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS growth and INDPRO stability shows negative or zero predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests over a 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 0.5 percent indicates an immediate supply shock that triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of instances where INDPRO drops by more than 0.5 percent month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low policy rate creates negative real yields that erode purchasing power and trigger tightening expectations, depressing S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, and the interaction effect is statistically insignificant at the 95 percent confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Steady payroll growth sustains baseline economic activity and corporate revenue stability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS level and growth falls below 0.01 over any 24-month rolling window, or signal loses predictive power during stable employment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding one million units signal broad construction and durable goods demand recovery, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of months where HOUST crosses above 1000, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth deceleration below 2 percent annualized triggers profit margin compression fears, causing investors to price in slower earnings and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 2.0 percent annualized, invalidating the earnings compression channel.
